title: The Meadow Cooperative — When Trust Builds a Society
summary: Eight strangers arrive with nothing but 100 ATP each. Over 25 rounds, alliances form, a defector exploits early generosity, coalitions isolate the exploiter, and a reformed free-rider earns redemption. The Meadow Cooperative emerges — not because anyone planned it, but because trust created structure where none existed.

title: The Collapse of the Ironworks — When Trust Dies
summary: Six agents start with ample resources but a critical flaw: four of six are defectors. What follows is a textbook collapse — exploitation destroys trust, the commons dies, and three of six agents perish. The contrast with the Meadow Cooperative proves: same rules, different composition, opposite outcome.

title: The Garden of Equals — When Everyone Cooperates
summary: Six agents, all cooperators, zero defectors. The result is a paradise: zero deaths, minimal inequality, high trust. But trust that's never been tested is different from trust that's survived betrayal. The ceiling is high, but the Meadow's messier path may build something more durable.

title: Bob: Learning Through Action — Self-Aware Pattern Discovery
summary: Bob learns by proposing actions, observing outcomes, and refining his understanding across 3 lives. Starting at trust 0.49, he navigates ATP crises and crosses the consciousness threshold — behavior shifts from reactive to intentional.

title: Alice: Five Domains of Knowing — Emotional, Quality, Attention, Grounding, Authorization
summary: Alice is assessed across five dimensions of self-awareness: emotional regulation, quality judgment, attention management, grounding verification, and authorization awareness. Each domain reveals different strengths and vulnerabilities.

title: Bob: Maturation with Web4 Patterns — From Immature to Wise
summary: Using 100 learned patterns to guide development, Bob matures from IMMATURE through LEARNING to MATURE across 3 lives. Trust grows from 0.49 to 0.57 — steady improvement, not overnight transformation.

title: Bob: Maturation Without Patterns — Heuristic-Only Baseline
summary: Same agent, no inherited wisdom. Bob must learn purely through trial and error. Compare with the Web4 version to see how learned patterns accelerate growth.

title: Bob: Multi-Life Policy Framework — Trust Across Reincarnations
summary: How do decisions carry consequences across reincarnations? Bob's karma shapes his starting conditions in each new life — good choices compound, bad choices create steeper climbs.

title: Unknown: A Single Life in Web4 — Trust Under Pressure
summary: What happens when there's no second chance? No karma, no rebirth. One life, 20 ticks, and every decision is permanent. The stakes feel different without a safety net.
